misconception: Oaky wines are low quality or over-extracted.
why_incorrect: Oak influence, when well-integrated and balanced, is a legitimate stylistic element rather than a quality defect. Classic Rioja Gran Reserva, top Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon, and White Burgundy all use significant oak contact and are considered benchmark quality wines. The issue is imbalance: when oak dominates fruit, or when cheap alternative oak products produce jarring flavours, the result may be poor quality. But the oak itself is not the quality problem — the lack of balance is.
corrected_understanding: Oak influence is a stylistic tool. When well-integrated with fruit, it adds complexity and structure. It becomes a quality problem only when imbalanced or poorly applied.
detection_signals: this wine smells of oak so it must be cheap
I can taste the oak so the wine is not natural

coaching_content.confusion_statement: You seem to be confusing: Oaky wines are low quality or over-extracted.
coaching_content.evidence_statement: This is based on your answers showing this pattern.
coaching_content.improvement_signal: Correctly explaining the relationship in similar questions
